errors: [The "Colonoscopy" expression checks if the count of procedures in the lookback period is equal to 0. This means the recommendation "Perform colon cancer screening" is triggered when NO colonoscopy has been performed. However, the lookback period is set to only 1 month ("value": 1, "unit": "month"). Clinically, colon cancer screening (colonoscopy) is typically recommended every 10 years. A 1-month lookback is clinically unreasonable as it would trigger a recommendation for a screening that was performed 2 months ago.]

[The "Assessment" logic is clinically incorrect. It uses a "Not Exists" operator on a "ProcedureLookBack" function with a lookback period of 1 month. This means the system will trigger a recommendation to perform prostate cancer screening if the patient has NOT had a screening procedure in the last 30 days. Clinically, prostate cancer screening (PSA, DRE) is not performed monthly; it is typically performed annually or every few years. A 1-month lookback is an inappropriately short interval for this clinical context.]

[The HbA1c threshold is set to 9 g%, which is clinically high for a general screening/diagnosis trigger. Standard clinical guidelines (e.g., ADA) typically use a threshold of 6.5% for the diagnosis of Diabetes Mellitus. A value of 9% is significantly above the diagnostic cutoff.]

The "Minimum Age" logic defines the age range as 60 to 75 years. Standard clinical guidelines for average-risk colorectal cancer screening typically recommend starting at age 45 (updated from 50) and continuing through age 75. The lower bound of 60 is clinically outdated and too high.

The age threshold in the "In Demographic" statement is set to 30 years (GreaterOrEqual 30). Standard clinical guidelines for general HIV screening typically target adults 13–64 years of age. Setting the minimum age to 30 excludes a significant portion of the recommended screening population.

[The BMI threshold for "Is Obese" is set to 35 kg/m2 in the ELM. Clinically, the threshold for obesity is typically 30 kg/m2. A value of 35 kg/m2 represents Class II obesity, which is more restrictive than standard screening guidelines for prediabetes/diabetes.]

[The "In Demographic" statement defines the age range as 30-65 years, but the "Rationale" statement explicitly mentions the USPSTF recommendation for women aged 21-65 years. This is a clinical inconsistency within the logic.]

[The 'In Demographic' statement defines the age threshold as 75 years or older (GreaterOrEqual 75), but the 'Rationale' statement explicitly references the USPSTF recommendation for adults 65 years or older. This creates a clinical mismatch between the logic implementation and the cited evidence.]

[The "In Demographic" statement requires the patient to be 25 years or older (GreaterOrEqual 25), but the "Rationale" statement explicitly mentions that the USPSTF recommends screening for adults 18 years or older. This is a clinical value mismatch.]

[The "In Demographic" statement defines the age range as 55-75 years, but the "Rationale" statement explicitly references the USPSTF recommendation for men aged 65-75 years. This is a clinical mismatch; the logic is triggering the alert 10 years earlier than the cited guideline.]

[The "MeetsInclusionCriteria" expression uses an "Or" operator between "Screening Age" (>= 40) and "Gender" (female). Clinically, breast cancer screening criteria should require BOTH age AND gender (an "And" operator) to be met. As currently written, any female under 40 or any person over 40 regardless of gender would trigger the inclusion criteria.]

The logic for "Needs Tobacco Use Screening" is incomplete. While the ELM defines a statement "Has Recent Tobacco Screening" (which checks for a tobacco screening within the last year), this statement is never referenced in the final logic. Currently, "InPopulation" only checks if the patient is 18+ ("In Demographic"), meaning the CDS will trigger a recommendation for every adult regardless of whether they have already been screened recently. Clinically, the recommendation should only trigger if the patient is 18+ AND does NOT have a recent tobacco screening.

[The "Needs AAA Screening" logic requires "In Demographic" and "Has Smoking History", but it fails to include the "Is Male" expression. The "Rationale" explicitly states the recommendation is for "men aged 65-75", but the ELM logic would trigger for any gender within that age range who has a smoking history.]

The logic for the "Colonoscopy" expression is clinically inverted. The ELM defines the population as patients who HAVE had a colonoscopy within the lookback period (Count > 0), and then triggers the recommendation "Perform colon cancer screening (colonoscopy)" for those same patients. Clinically, a screening recommendation should be triggered for patients who have NOT had the procedure within the recommended interval.

The logic for "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" is clinically contradictory. The ELM requires the patient to be $\ge 65$ AND to already have a "Recent Falls Assessment" (within the last year) to trigger the recommendation to "Perform falls risk assessment." Clinically, a screening alert should trigger if the patient is in the demographic AND has NOT had a recent assessment.

[The expression for "MeetsExclusionCriteria" (localId 537) is implemented as an "And" type in the ELM, but the clinical logic described in the annotations and the rationale indicates it should be an "Or" (Connective Tissue Disorders OR Amiodarones). This creates a logic mismatch where a patient would only be excluded if they had BOTH conditions, rather than either.]
